name: Mark Murphy
abstract: Mark Howe Murphy (March 14, 1932 – October 22, 2015) was an American jazz singer based at various times in New York City, Los Angeles, London, and San Francisco. He recorded 51 albums under his own name during his lifetime and was principally known for his innovative vocal improvisations.

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Celebrates Nat King Cole
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: So Mark was on it―Mark Murphy, myself, Dee Dee Bridgewater and Don Cherry. And Howard Johnson, you know. So he had all of… George Gruntz had a way with really knowing who he wanted, and um, he had all of us come over and we rehearsed this jazz opera
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: Mark Murphy taught me a long time ago... Mark would let me sit in... so you learn how to stay out of the way but enhance the melody... I got a kind of a style from Mark Murphy
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: Mark Murphy has been a wonderful friend and I love the way he sings and I’m happy to say he likes the way I sing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: when I worked with Mark Murphy or when I worked with Ann Hampton Callaway as an arranger for their records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: Mark Murphy was one of the 13 singers we hired to do Sing a Song of Basie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Gilmore
about_quotes.evidence: the night before that I’m playing with Mark Murphy, a one- nighter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Gilmore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999
